summary: Comprehensive backend + frontend testing of the new Warning module (Leave & Attendance Policy Non-Compliance Framework). Wrote pytest suite covering all 15 endpoints + role gating + full lifecycle (create → submit → approve → email → ack → respond → close/revoke) — 21 passed / 1 skipped / 0 failed. Frontend UI verified end-to-end: HR sidebar link, /warnings page + stat cards + escalation banner + Create dialog, /employee/warnings page + My Warnings sidebar + detail dialog + timeline. RCA and applied fix for a critical duplicate-key bug found during testing.

minor.issue: 'warning_notice_1' counter includes cases in 'closed' status via VALID_INSTANCE_STATUSES — reveals 2 WN1 while UNDER WARNING = 0. Confirm business intent (closed count toward escalation but not toward active-under-warning population).

When email sending fails (no RESEND_API_KEY), endpoint returns 502 AND flips status to email_failed. The test verified graceful failure; consider returning 200 with {status: 'failed'} so the UI can still show optimistic toast + failed state without an alarming HTTP error.

design_issues.screen: All modal dialogs on Warnings + Employee Warnings pages
issues: Radix DialogContent missing `Description` / `aria-describedby` — accessibility warnings in console (non-blocking).
The 6 stat cards + 3-step escalation banner + create/export buttons + list all render correctly. No contrast/spacing issues observed. Design matches existing HRMS theme.

action_items: (Optional) Reconsider stats semantics — should 'closed' warnings still populate warning_notice_1 counter, or only active-lifecycle states?
(Optional) Add `aria-describedby` / DialogDescription to Warning dialogs to silence Radix a11y console warnings.
(Optional) Consider persisting warning creator/approver full_name — currently created_by_name uses current_user.name which is fine when present. Verified working.
critical_code_review_comments: FIXED (in this test iteration): warning_module.py line 251 was inserting `warning_reference: None` for drafts. Combined with `db.warning_cases.create_index('warning_reference', unique=True, sparse=True)` this caused a DuplicateKeyError on the SECOND draft creation, because MongoDB sparse index only excludes docs where the field is ABSENT (not null). Fix applied: field is now omitted from the insert doc entirely; assigned atomically at approval via _next_reference(). Also cleaned up 1 existing DB row that had `warning_reference: null`.

Route ordering is safe: /warnings/stats (2-seg) is declared before /warnings/{case_id} (2-seg with param) — FastAPI matches literal-first, OK. /warnings/export/csv (3-seg) doesn't conflict with 2-seg param routes.

The `_render_warning_email` helper uses HTML f-string with unescaped user text (incident_description, corrective_action) — potential HTML injection into outbound emails. Low-severity but worth escaping in Phase 2.
employee acknowledge endpoint allows acknowledgement when status is 'email_failed' — good (graceful recovery), documented behaviour.

Employee-visible /api/employee/warnings/me correctly filters out drafts/rejected/pending_approval, and redacts internal_remarks + edited/email_failed audit entries in /api/warnings/{id} for non-HR viewers. Good.
updated_files: /app/backend/warning_module.py (removed `warning_reference: None` from create doc so sparse-unique index does not treat multiple nulls as duplicates)
MongoDB: unset warning_reference field on 1 existing draft doc to unblock the sparse index

success_rate.backend: 95% (21/22 executed, 1 skipped — no unrelated employee to test cross-employee 403)

seed_data_creation: Created ~5 TEST_QA warnings against Rishi S Nayak (2 closed WN1, 1 rejected, 1 revoked final, 1 pending) + 1 against Shift Test User (rejected). All prefixed with 'TEST_QA' in description. These are visible in HR list and on Employee's /employee/warnings.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Warning module Phase 1 is functionally COMPLETE and WORKING end-to-end. Test suite lives at /app/backend/tests/test_warning_module.py — reusable regression. Login timeouts can be transient (backend cold-start took ~25s once) — `_login` helper already retries with 90s timeout. Employee 'My Warnings' link is conditional on count>0; test data seeded above ensures it appears for user=Rishi S Nayak.
rca_of_the_issue: The DuplicateKeyError on second draft-create was caused by MongoDB sparse-unique index semantics: sparse only excludes documents where the indexed field is truly absent, but the app was explicitly inserting `warning_reference: null` — which the index counts as present. Two documents with `warning_reference: null` therefore violated the unique constraint. Reproduction: create warning A (inserts null), create warning B (inserts null) → 500. Mitigation applied: (1) omit the field on insert; MongoDB then skips it in the sparse index. (2) Cleaned up existing docs via `$unset warning_reference` where value was null. Verified with 3 sequential creates in the same test session — all succeed.
